verses.1: But Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
verses.2: Early in the morning He went back into the temple courts.
verses.3: The scribes and Pharisees, however, brought to Him a woman caught in adultery. They made her stand before them
verses.4: and said, "Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of adultery.
verses.5: In the Law Moses commanded us to stone such a woman. So what do You say?"
verses.6: They said this to test Him, in order to have a basis for accusing Him. But Jesus bent down and began to write on the ground with His finger.

segments.text: When they continued to question Him, He straightened up and said to them,
"Let him who is without sin among you be the first to cast a stone at her."
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.8: And again He bent down and wrote on the ground.
verses.9: When they heard this, they began to go away one by one, starting with the older ones, until only Jesus was left, with the woman standing there before Him.

segments.text: Then Jesus straightened up and asked her,
"Woman, where are your accusers? Has no one condemned you?"
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: "No one, Lord," she answered.
"Then neither do I condemn you," Jesus declared. "Now go and sin no more."
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Once again, Jesus spoke to the people and said,
"I am the light of the world. Whoever follows Me will never walk in the darkness, but will have the light of life."
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.13: So the Pharisees said to Him, "You are testifying about Yourself; Your testimony is not valid."

segments.text: Jesus replied,
"Even if I testify about Myself, My testimony is valid, because I know where I came from and where I am going. But you do not know where I came from or where I am going.
segments.speaker: Jesus

15.text: You judge according to the flesh; I judge no one.
15.speaker: Jesus

16.text: But even if I do judge, My judgment is true, because I am not alone; I am with the Father who sent Me.
16.speaker: Jesus

17.text: Even in your own Law it is written that the testimony of two men is valid.
17.speaker: Jesus

18.text: I am One who testifies about Myself, and the Father, who sent Me, also testifies about Me."
18.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: "Where is Your Father?" they asked Him.
"You do not know Me or My Father," Jesus answered. "If you knew Me, you would know My Father as well."
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.20: He spoke these words while teaching in the temple courts, near the treasury. Yet no one seized Him, because His hour had not yet come.

segments.text: Again He said to them,
"I am going away, and you will look for Me, but you will die in your sin. Where I am going, you cannot come."
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.22: So the Jews began to ask, "Will He kill Himself, since He says, 'Where I am going, you cannot come'?"

segments.text: Then He told them,
"You are from below; I am from above. You are of this world; I am not of this world.
segments.speaker: Jesus

24.text: That is why I told you that you would die in your sins. For unless you believe that I am He, you will die in your sins."
24.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: "Who are You?" they asked.
"Just what I have been telling you from the beginning," Jesus replied.
segments.speaker: Jesus

26.text: "I have much to say about you and much to judge. But the One who sent Me is truthful, and what I have heard from Him, I tell the world."
26.speaker: Jesus

verses.27: They did not understand that He was telling them about the Father.

segments.text: So Jesus said,
"When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am He, and that I do nothing on My own, but speak exactly what the Father has taught Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

29.text: He who sent Me is with Me. He has not left Me alone, because I always do what pleases Him."
29.speaker: Jesus

verses.30: As Jesus spoke these things, many believed in Him.

segments.text: So He said to the Jews who had believed Him,
"If you continue in My word, you are truly My disciples.
segments.speaker: Jesus

32.text: Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free."
32.speaker: Jesus

verses.33: "We are Abraham's descendants," they answered. "We have never been slaves to anyone. How can You say we will be set free?"

segments.text: Jesus replied,
"Truly, truly, I tell you, everyone who sins is a slave to sin.
segments.speaker: Jesus

35.text: A slave does not remain in the house forever, but a son remains forever.
35.speaker: Jesus

36.text: So if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.
36.speaker: Jesus

37.text: I know you are Abraham's descendants, but you are trying to kill Me because My word has no place within you.
37.speaker: Jesus

38.text: I speak of what I have seen in the presence of the Father, and you do what you have heard from your father."
38.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: "Abraham is our father," they replied.
"If you were children of Abraham," said Jesus, "you would do the works of Abraham.
segments.speaker: Jesus

40.text: But now you are trying to kill Me, a man who has told you the truth that I heard from God. Abraham never did such a thing.
40.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: "You are doing the works of your father."
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: "We are not illegitimate children," they declared. "Our only Father is God Himself."
Jesus said to them,
"If God were your Father, you would love Me, for I have come here from God. I have not come on My own, but He sent Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

43.text: Why do you not understand what I am saying? It is because you are unable to accept My message.
43.speaker: Jesus

44.text: You belong to your father, the devil, and you want to carry out his desires. He was a murderer from the beginning, refusing to uphold the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks his native language, because he is a liar and the father of lies.
44.speaker: Jesus

45.text: But because I speak the truth, you do not believe Me!
45.speaker: Jesus

46.text: Which of you can prove Me guilty of sin? If I speak the truth, why do you not believe Me?
46.speaker: Jesus

47.text: Whoever belongs to God hears the words of God. The reason you do not hear is that you do not belong to God."
47.speaker: Jesus

verses.48: The Jews answered Him, "Are we not right to say that You are a Samaritan and You have a demon?"

segments.text: "I do not have a demon," Jesus replied,
"but I honor My Father, and you dishonor Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

50.text: I do not seek My own glory. There is One who seeks it, and He is the Judge.
50.speaker: Jesus

51.text: Truly, truly, I tell you, if anyone keeps My word, he will never see death."
51.speaker: Jesus

verses.52: "Now we know that You have a demon!" declared the Jews. "Abraham died, and so did the prophets, yet You say that anyone who keeps Your word will never taste death.
verses.53: Are You greater than our father Abraham? He died, as did the prophets. Who do You claim to be?"

segments.text: Jesus answered,
"If I glorify Myself, My glory means nothing. The One who glorifies Me is My Father, of whom you say, 'He is our God.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

55.text: You do not know Him, but I know Him. If I said I did not know Him, I would be a liar like you. But I do know Him, and I keep His word.
55.speaker: Jesus

56.text: Your father Abraham rejoiced that he would see My day. He saw it and was glad."
56.speaker: Jesus

verses.57: Then the Jews said to Him, "You are not yet fifty years old, and You have seen Abraham?"

segments.text: "Truly, truly, I tell you," Jesus declared,
"before Abraham was born, I am!"
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.59: At this, they picked up stones to throw at Him. But Jesus was hidden and went out of the temple area.

metadata.source: Derived from the Berean Standard Bible (BSB). Missing verses restored from the Textus Receptus tradition for completeness.
